name: Fuente Obejuna, Belmez, Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo – ruta circular
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Fuente Obejuna offer diverse landscapes in the Alto Guadiato region of Córdoba, Spain. The terrain features scenic oak pastures, hills, and the Guadiato River, providing varied routes. Trails range from moderate rides suitable for all skill levels to hard mountain bike routes with significant elevation gains, catering to both less experienced and advanced riders. The area includes historical landmarks like Belmez Castle and the Parish Church Nuestra Señora del Castillo, adding cultural interest to outdoor excursions. Best mountain bike trails in Fuente Obejuna The most popular mountain bike trail is Fuente Obejuna, Belmez, Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo – ruta circular, a 43.2 miles (69.5 km) trail that takes 5 hours 12 minutes to complete. This difficult route connects several towns, offering a comprehensive exploration of the region. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is View of Fuente Obejuna – Fuente Obejuna Town Center loop from Fuente Obejuna, a difficult 35.0 miles (56.3 km) path. This loop provides expansive views of Fuente Obejuna and its surroundings. Local mountain bikers also love the Puerto de Martín Alonso loop from Argallón, a 60.9 miles (98.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 7 hours 48 minutes. This challenging route features significant elevation changes. Mountain biking around Fuente Obejuna is defined by scenic oak pastures, the Guadiato River, and historical landmarks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult routes. The routes in Fuente Obejuna are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 150 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Fuente Obejuna's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Fuente Obejuna?
A: There are over 25 mountain bike trails around Fuente Obejuna, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The komoot community has rated these routes with an average score of 4.0 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Fuente Obejuna?
A: The terrain around Fuente Obejuna is diverse, featuring scenic oak pastures, rolling hills, and routes along the Guadiato River. You'll encounter a mix of agricultural land and natural waterways, providing varied landscapes for your ride.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Fuente Obejuna?
A: Yes, there are mountain bike trails suitable for less experienced riders. While many routes are moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy trail available, corresponding to STS S0-S1 for those looking for a gentler ride.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders in Fuente Obejuna?
A: Absolutely. Fuente Obejuna offers several difficult mountain bike routes, with some featuring significant elevation gains up to 3,000 feet. These trails, corresponding to STS S2, S3-S6, demand very good fitness and advanced riding skills. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988429550/fuente-obejuna-belmez-penarroya-pueblonuevo-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Obejuna, Belmez, Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844113" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Martín Alonso loop from Argallón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical landmarks or points of interest can I see while mountain biking in Fuente Obejuna?
A: The region is rich in history. You can explore the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5069892" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Obejuna Town Center&lt;/a&gt;, known for its narrow, steep streets. Overlooking the area is the 13th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5360266" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belmez Castle&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5354209" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Our Lady of Grace&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5316017" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument to the Terrible Dog and Statue of the Miner of Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo&lt;/a&gt; are also notable attractions.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Fuente Obejuna?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Fuente Obejuna are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988429550/fuente-obejuna-belmez-penarroya-pueblonuevo-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Obejuna, Belmez, Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844113" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Martín Alonso loop from Argallón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Fuente Obejuna?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from scenic oak pastures to routes along the Guadiato River, and the variety of trails catering to different skill levels.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Fuente Obejuna?
A: While many routes are moderate to difficult, there are options for families. Look for trails described as 'easy' or those with lower elevation gains and shorter distances for a more relaxed family outing. Always check the route details to ensure it matches your family's skill level.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Fuente Obejuna?
A: The region experiences a Mediterranean climate. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential chill of winter. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: Can I find routes that offer expansive views of the region?
A: Yes, several routes provide stunning panoramic views. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122264" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Fuente Obejuna – Fuente Obejuna Town Center loop from Fuente Obejuna&lt;/a&gt; offers expansive vistas of Fuente Obejuna and its surroundings. Additionally, landmarks like Belmez Castle provide excellent viewpoints.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes that pass through or near the Fuente Obejuna Town Center?
A: Yes, some routes are designed to incorporate the town center. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31845519" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Obejuna Town Center loop from Los Pánchez&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that allows you to experience the town's historical ambiance while mountain biking.
tips: Very close to the town, remains of what was once a Roman city called Mellaria (city of honey) have been found.

The current municipality and its name are the result of the merger in 1927 of the two municipalities: Peñarroya and Pueblonuevo del Terrible. The name of Pueblonuevo del Terrible is given to a famous dog called "Terrible" who was feared for his ferocity and who lived in the "new town". There is a legend about him, in which it is said that he was the one who discovered coal in lands close to the town.

Belmez Castle is a historic fortress located in Belmez. It stands majestically on a high limestone rock, offering a stunning view of the village and surrounding areas.
The castle dates back to at least 1245, with significant additions made in the 15th century. It played an important role during the Reconquista and was occupied by French troops during the Peninsular War (1810-1812). The castle features a pentagonal Keep, six semicircular towers and a zigzag staircase leading up to it.
If you're planning a visit, it's open daily and admission is free.
